mission.title: Install and Configure Kitops on Kubernetes
mission.description: KitOps is an open source DevOps tool for packaging and versioning AI/ML models, datasets, code, and configuration into an OCI Artifact. Installing KitOps allows you to manage your AI/ML projects efficiently and securely, leveraging the power of the CNCF ecosystem.

steps.title: Add the KitOps Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official KitOps Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kitops https://charts.kitops.org
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KitOps using Helm
steps.description:
Install KitOps in a new namespace called 'kitops' with the following command:
```bash
helm install kitops kitops/kitops --namespace kitops --create-namespace --version v1.11.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the KitOps pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kitops
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the KitOps deployment to set resource limits. Create a YAML file named `kitops-resources.yaml`:
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: kitops
  namespace: kitops
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: kitops
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "512Mi"
``` 
Then apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kitops-resources.yaml
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward the KitOps service to your local machine to access the UI:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/kitops --namespace kitops 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KitOps will have the KitOps pods running in the 'kitops' namespace, and you will be able to access the KitOps UI at http://localhost:8080. Resource limits will be configured as specified.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kitops https://charts.kitops.org
helm repo update
helm install kitops kitops/kitops --namespace kitops --create-namespace --version v1.11.0

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: kitops
  namespace: kitops
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: kitops
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "512Mi"

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
